that only would work on a deserted island, right? Because like we have TV, we have neighbors who say, what? You're doing what? What century are you from, girl? They hate plate liquors. Plate liquors are people who come to the seminars for the free meal. And then pretty much were unqualified or had no intention of what kind of part. But they go to all these seminars, by the way. So seniors, which is who they're selling to. The first phenomenon you might want to know about seniors that you don't know, the next time you're in a casino, watch for this. There are seniors who haunt the casinos with a rewards card. And they ask to put it in your slot machine if you don't have a rewards card, because they are eating off the points. This is their supplement to social security. This is their retirement plan. They're in the casino all day stacking up reward points on other people's play. So they're mostly over at the dollar machines, by the way. They're not at the penny machines. And they're eating off the points. And Trump casinos in Atlantic City are the most generous. It's like 10 points to $1 or something, and you can go into buffet and use them. So really, she's only got to work for a few hours, and she can get a good meal. So there's seniors. So there are a bunch of seniors who go to all these seminars just to eat. I mean, there's at least a couple of them a week, so it's the night out. I mean, they go, and they have a nice meal. And some of them are feeding them steak for God's sake. So they all hate. They use the term, and they hate them. Plate-leckers. I hate plate-leckers. Now, it never occurs to most of them. There's a simple solution to this. Don't feed them. Simple. Now, you'll have less people in the room, but so what? You should hate them. Why do you want them in the room? You don't want a person in the room who came for the meal. Guess what the 4x6 card is? It has to be turned into 12 audio CDs in three days of training. Don't feed them. That's it. I just gave it to you. And they're loving it. That's full replacement. So you step down, though, as reinvention. So reinvention structure is essentially not as radical. So like to go back to our cash practice example, that's too radical. That's true replacement. We're never going to take insurance again. We're not going to take insurance from anybody. We're not going to take a Medicare patient. We're not going to take a Medicaid patient. And we're only going to do cash. And we're going to do prepay. That's true replacement. So reinvention would be more about maybe how to only get the patients who have really good insurance. That might be reinvention.
